_description: Based on Bouman, Steg & Kiers (2018, Frontiers in Psychology). Altruistic value orientation: concern for the welfare and wellbeing of other human beings and society at large. These individuals act pro-environmentally when doing so also benefits others (e.g. reducing congestion, improving air quality). They favour shared, collective transport options. E-SVS altruistic items: equality, a world at peace, social justice, helpful, caring for the weak.

_value_rationale.pro_environment: High — altruistic values correlate positively with pro-environmental beliefs when the two align (Bouman et al. 2018; De Groot & Steg 2007); pro-environmental behaviour benefits others
_value_rationale.physical_activity: Moderate — active travel acceptable; not a primary driver but consistent with social norms
_value_rationale.privacy: Very low — strongly prefers collective/shared modes; PT aligns with social orientation
_value_rationale.autonomy: Very low — willingly defers to shared schedules; autonomy is not a priority for other-focused individuals
_value_rationale.hedonism: Low — pleasure/comfort is subordinated to social benefit (hedonic and altruistic values sit on opposing dimensions in Schwartz's circular model)
_value_rationale.cost_saving: Moderate-high — cost sensitivity reflects awareness of economic equity and fairness to others
_value_rationale.speed: Low-moderate — willing to accept slower journeys when they benefit the collective (e.g. reducing road congestion)
_value_rationale.safety: Very high — safety of self and others is paramount; aligns directly with altruistic concern for wellbeing
_value_rationale.comfort: Moderate — moderate comfort expected but not at the expense of collective benefit
